mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, causing equity valuations to decline over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the t-statistic for return difference is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without inflationary pressure, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in greater than 50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during recovery phases, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical edge when INDPRO growth accelerates.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal increased construction activity and consumer confidence in durable goods, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts growth fails to predict equity direction with an information coefficient above 0.04 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly inflation accelerates beyond 0.4%, markets price in aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in greater than 65% of high-inflation months, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment confirms broad-based labor market strength, supporting corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal yields negative returns in greater than 55% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for macro growth.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating quarterly GDP growth indicates weakening aggregate demand and corporate revenue pressure, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP deceleration coincides with positive equity returns in greater than 60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power during late-cycle phases.
